messages.content:
rewrite this for ETFs instead of Pharmaceutical companies:
فصل مقدمه یک پایان نامه نقش مهمی در تنظیم زمینه برای مطالعه دارد. هدف این فصل ارائه یک نمای کلی از پیشینه، منطق، سؤالات تحقیق و اهداف است. همچنین اهمیت تحقیق و پیامدهای بالقوه یافته‌های مطالعه را برجسته می‌کند.
در چارچوب ارزیابی شرکت های دارویی بورس اوراق بهادار تهران با استفاده از تحلیل پوششی داده های شبکه، فصل مقدمه به عنوان دروازه‌ای برای تحقیق عمل می‌کند، مقدمه‌ای برای موضوع ارائه می‌دهد و زمینه را برای فصل های بعدی فراهم می‌کند.
فصل مقدمه با معرفی موضوع تحقیق و ارائه پیشینه مختصری از صنعت داروسازی در ایران آغاز می شود. سپس اهمیت ارزیابی عملکرد شرکت‌های دارویی پذیرفته شده در بورس اوراق بهادار تهران را برجسته می‌کند. سپس این فصل به تشریح سوالات و اهداف تحقیق می پردازد و چارچوب روشنی برای مطالعه ارائه می دهد. همچنین روشی را که مطالعه به کار خواهد گرفت، از جمله تحلیل پوششی داده های شبکه، برجسته می کند.
در نهایت، فصل مقدمه با برجسته کردن اهمیت تحقیق، هم از نظر پیشبرد زمینه ارزیابی عملکرد در صنعت داروسازی و هم کمک به مجموعه گسترده‌تر دانش در مورد تحلیل پوششی داده‌های شبکه، به پایان می‌رسد.
به طور کلی، فصل مقدمه این پایان نامه مروری واضح و مختصر از پیشینه، منطق، سؤالات تحقیق و اهداف تحقیق ارائه می دهد. این فصل زمینه را برای فصل های بعدی آماده می کند.

فصل مقدمه یک پایان نامه نقش بنیادینی در ایجاد بستر مناسب برای مطالعه ایفا می‌کند. هدف از این فصل ارائه یک دید کلی از پیشینه، مبانی منطقی، سوالات تحقیقی و اهداف مطالعه است. برجسته‌سازی اهمیت تحقیق و تأثیرات احتمالی نتایج آن نیز از دیگر مقاصد این بخش به شمار می‌رود.

در راستای بررسی صندوق‌های قابل معامله در بورس (ETF)، فصل مقدمه همچون درگاهی جهت پی بردن به موضوع تحقیق است، که یک مقدمه‌ای بر موضوع پیش‌رو ارائه داده و برای فصل‌های بعدی زمینه‌سازی می‌کند.

این فصل با معرفی موضوع تحقیق و ارائه پیش‌زمینه‌ای اجمالی از بازار ETFها آغاز می‌شود. سپس به تبیین اهمیت ارزیابی عملکرد صندوق‌های قابل معامله در بورس اوراق بهادار و تأکید بر آن می‌پردازد. فصل سپس به تشریح سوالات تحقیقی و اهداف مطالعه پرداخته و چهارچوب روشنی برای تحقیق ارائه می‌دهد. همچنین، روش‌هایی که در مطالعه به‌کار رفته‌اند، مانند تحلیل پوششی داده‌های شبکه‌ای، مد نظر قرار می‌گیرد.

در نهایت، فصل مقدمه با تأکید بر اهمیت تحقیق، هم از دید پیشبرد دانش در زمینه ارزیابی عملکرد بازار ETFها و هم از دید کمک به دایره وسیع‌تری از معرفت مرتبط با تحلیل‌های پوششی داده‌های شبکه به پایان می‌رسد.

به طور کلی، فصل مقدمه این پایان‌نامه به بررسی دقیق و خلاصه‌ای از پیشینه، منطق، سوالات تحقیقی و اهداف تحقیق اختصاص دارد. این فصل بنیانی مناسب برای فصل‌های پیش‌رو فراهم می‌آورد.

give a brief introduction for chapter 1 (introduction chapter) of a thesis with this title:
Performance Evaluation of Exchange Traded Funds of Tehran Stock Exchange using Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) and Range Adjusted Measure (RAM): A Super-Efficiency Approach

Chapter 1: Introduction

The opening chapter of any dissertation sets the tone for the entire study by elucidating the research background, defining the research questions and objectives, and highlighting the significance of the research within the broader academic and professional context. The purpose of this introductory chapter is to lay the foundation for our exploration into the performance of Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) on the Tehran Stock Exchange using a robust methodological framework.

This thesis, titled "Performance Evaluation of Exchange Traded Funds of Tehran Stock Exchange using Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) and Range Adjusted Measure (RAM): A Super-Efficiency Approach," aims to provide a comprehensive analysis of the efficiency and performance of ETFs in the capital market of Iran. 

In this chapter, we will offer an overview of the Exchange Traded Funds mechanism, delve into their emergence in the Tehran Stock Exchange, and outline the evolution and characteristics that make ETFs a unique investment instrument in the Iranian financial market. We will then move on to delineate the significance of employing advanced analytical techniques such as DEA and RAM for evaluating the performance of financial instruments, highlighting the research gap that this study intends to bridge.

Furthermore, the research questions will be precisely posed, followed by the exposition of the study's objectives designed to address these inquiries. We will also cover the scope and limitations inherent in this research, ensuring a clear understanding of the contexts within which the findings of this thesis should be interpreted.

Finally, we set the stage for the subsequent chapters, which will explore the literature review, methodology, results, and discussions, culminating in conclusions and recommendations derived from our findings. Through this investigation, we aim not only to shed light on the performance of ETFs in the Tehran Stock Exchange but also to contribute to the existing body of knowledge on efficiency measurement in the realm of finance using super-efficiency models.

summarize this:
این فصل مروری جامع بر تحقیقات و مطالعات موجود در زمینه شرکت های دارویی، بورس اوراق بهادار تهران، تحلیل پوششی داده های شبکه و کاربرد آنها در ارزیابی عملکرد شرکت های دارویی پذیرفته شده در بورس اوراق بهادار تهران ارائه می‌کند.
هدف این فصل مشخص کردن شکاف تحقیق و سؤالات تحقیقی است که مطالعه قصد دارد به آنها بپردازد. این فصل همچنین اهمیت ارزیابی عملکرد شرکت‌های دارویی در بورس اوراق بهادار تهران، با توجه به نقش حیاتی آنها در اقتصاد و لزوم اطمینان از عملکرد کارآمد و مؤثر آنها را برجسته می‌کند. مرور ادبیات تحلیلی انتقادی از ادبیات موجود در مورد ارزیابی شرکت‌های دارویی، از جمله معیارهای عملکرد مالی و غیر مالی آنها ارائه می‌کند. همچنین مفهوم تحلیل پوششی داده‌های شبکه، یک تکنیک قدرتمند برای اندازه‌گیری عملکرد شرکت‌ها در یک شبکه را مورد بحث قرار می‌دهد.
به طور کلی، فصل مرور ادبیات با هدف ارائه درک جامعی از مفاهیم کلیدی، نظریه‌ها و روش‌شناسی مرتبط با سؤالات تحقیق مطالعه است. همچنین شکاف‌های موجود در ادبیات و نیاز به تحقیقات بیشتر در زمینه ارزیابی عملکرد شرکت‌های دارویی با استفاده از تحلیل پوششی داده‌های شبکه را برجسته می‌کند.

This chapter provides a thorough overview of existing research on pharmaceutical companies, the Tehran Stock Exchange, network data envelopment analysis (DEA), and their application in performance evaluation of pharmaceutical companies listed on the Tehran Stock Exchange. The aim of the chapter is to identify research gaps and formulate research questions that the study intends to address. It emphasizes the importance of performance evaluation for pharmaceutical companies in the Tehran Stock Exchange, given their vital role in the economy and the necessity for ensuring their efficient and effective functioning. A critical analytic review of the literature on the performance evaluation of pharmaceutical companies, including both financial and non-financial performance metrics, is presented. Additionally, the concept of network DEA, a powerful technique for measuring the performance of companies within a network, is discussed. Overall, the literature review chapter aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of the key concepts, theories, and methodologies related to the research questions of the study. It also highlights the existing gaps in the literature and the need for further research in the area of performance evaluation of pharmaceutical companies using network DEA.
explain about the status, mechanisms and history of ETFs in Iran

Status of ETFs in Iran:

Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) in Iran are a relatively recent addition to the investment landscape, having been introduced in the country in the early 2010s. The popularity of ETFs in Iran has grown, as they provide a convenient way for investors to diversify their portfolios while enjoying the benefits of both mutual funds and stocks. As of my knowledge cutoff in 2023, there are various ETFs available to investors on the Tehran Stock Exchange (TSE) and Iran Fara Bourse (IFB), which cover a range of asset classes including stocks, bonds, and commodities.

Mechanisms of ETFs:

An ETF is a type of investment fund that is traded on stock exchanges, much like a stock. An ETF holds assets such as stocks, bonds, commodities, or a basket of assets. Unlike mutual funds, shares of ETFs are bought and sold during the day at market price, which can be more or less than the fund's net asset value (NAV). Investors can buy and sell ETF units through their brokerage accounts, and the liquidity of ETFs makes them an attractive option for many.

ETFs in Iran typically employ either a physical replication strategy, where the fund holds all (or a substantial portion of) the assets it is tracking in the underlying index, or a synthetic replication strategy, involving the use of financial derivatives to achieve the same investment goals.

History of ETFs in Iran:

ETFs were introduced in Iran as part of the capital market's development plan to provide diverse financial instruments to investors. The first Iranian ETF was launched to offer investors exposure to a portfolio of leading TSE-listed companies. The introduction of ETFs in Iran aimed to facilitate greater market participation by retail investors and offer new tools for financial planning and portfolio management.

The development of ETFs in Iran faced challenges related to the regulatory framework, investor education, and market infrastructure. Nevertheless, the authorities continued to support the growth of ETFs by introducing new regulations to help ensure transparency, protect investors, and provide tax advantages.

The ETF market in Iran has gradually expanded to include funds with various investment focuses, including blue-chip stocks, government bonds, and sector-specific assets. Furthermore, to help stimulate economic growth and offset state budget deficits, the Iranian government has also used ETFs to privatize some state-owned enterprises, offering shares to the public through ETF structures.

Interest in ETFs within the Iranian investment community is likely influenced by factors such as ease of access, lower fees compared to traditional mutual funds, and the ability to trade in real-time. The increased flexibility and the ability to take quick positions in response to market movements make ETFs a favorable tool amongst Iranian investors, mirroring global trends in the growing popularity of ETFs.
explain about the status, mechanisms and history of ETFs in Iran in full details

The Presence of Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) in Iran's financial market signifies a step towards sophisticated investment vehicles paralleling global trends, albeit within the unique contours of its economy and regulatory environment.

Status of ETFs in Iran:
ETFs in Iran have burgeoned since their inception, offering a range of asset exposures to investors. The Tehran Stock Exchange (TSE) and Iran Fara Bourse (IFB) accommodate numerous ETFs, covering equities, fixed income, and various sectorial assets. These funds have been increasingly recognized for portfolio diversification and as an alternative to direct stock investments, appealing to both retail and institutional investors. ETF assets under management and the variety of such products have been growing, catering to an evolving appetite for broader market participation and improved investment strategies. The market for ETFs also gained momentum as the Iranian government took steps for privatization and economic stimuli, facilitating it through ETFs.

Mechanisms of ETFs:
ETFs operate similarly in Iran as they do internationally, but with adherence to Iran’s financial regulations and economic context. An ETF is typically a basket representing an index, sector, commodity, or a basket of assets that trades on an exchange. The vehicle enables investors to purchase or sell fund units throughout the trading day priced at current market rates.

Investors engage with ETFs on the TSE or IFB through licensed brokerage firms. Orders for ETF units can be placed during trading hours, providing real-time pricing and the ability to respond swiftly to market changes. In terms of the creation and redemption process, Authorized Participants (APs) in Iran—usually large financial institutions—interact directly with the ETF provider to manage the supply of ETF units, facilitating the alignment of the ETF price with its NAV.

The accessibility of ETFs is underscored by their contrast with mutual funds, which are priced once at the end of a trading day. ETFs also often entail lower expense ratios than mutual funds, an attribute that can be enticing for cost-conscious investors.

History of ETFs in Iran:
ETFs were introduced in Iran as a mechanism to provide greater sophistication in financial instruments and to encourage broader market participation. The first ETF in Iran was launched in the early 2010s, marking an essential milestone for the Iranian capital market. Following the first introduction, both the volume and diversity of ETF offerings have expanded, reflecting a maturation process shaped by regulatory enhancements and growing investor interest.

The history of ETFs traces the trajectory of Iran's intention to diversify its financial offerings while tackling economic challenges, such as state-owned enterprise privatization. The ETF framework has thus been not only a gateway for private investment but also a tool for public financial management.

The maturity of ETFs in Iran is shadowed by several challenges, chiefly, regulatory frameworks that require continued refinement, the need for investor education regarding ETF operations, and infrastructure that ensures efficient market operations. National regulations pertaining to transparency and investor protection, as well as tax considerations, are ongoing areas of development.

Today, Iranian ETFs span an array of investment focuses and are paving the way for both domestic and potentially international investors (where sanctions and regulations permit) to gain exposure to Iranian equities, bonds, and other asset classes wrapped within an ETF structure. The education of potential investors and the promotion of market literacy concerning ETFs are increasingly seen as pivotal to consolidating their place within Iran's financial market landscape. 

In summary, the status, mechanisms, and history of ETFs in Iran reflect a vibrant segment of the market that has evolved within the country's specific economic and regulatory context. While growing steadily, this segment's future trajectory will likely continue to be influenced by both domestic economic policies and the overall global appetite for diversified investment products.
Explain about importance and methods of performance evaluation of ETFs in full details

Importance of Performance Evaluation of ETFs:

Performance evaluation of Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) is critical for investors, fund managers, and the overall market ecosystem. It provides valuable insights into how effectively an ETF is achieving its stated objectives, such as tracking a particular index or outperforming a benchmark. The importance of performance evaluation for ETFs includes the following aspects:

1. **Benchmark Tracking:** ETFs commonly aim to replicate the performance of a specific index. Assessing how closely an ETF follows its benchmark index is fundamental in determining its tracking efficiency.

2. **Risk Management:** Performance evaluation enables investors to understand the risk profile of an ETF. It sheds light on the ETF's volatility and other risk metrics relative to its returns.

3. **Investment Decision Making:** Investors can make informed decisions based on the performance analysis of ETFs by comparing historical returns, fees, liquidity, and risk-adjusted performance across different funds.

4. **Portfolio Diversification:** Evaluating the performance of various ETFs helps investors build and maintain a diversified portfolio aligned with their investment goals and risk tolerance.

5. **Fee Justification:** Management fees and other costs can erode returns. Analyzing performance in the context of costs ensures that investors are receiving value for the fees associated with an ETF.

6. **Regulatory Compliance:** Regulators may require periodic performance reporting to ensure transparency and protect investors' interests, making performance evaluation a compliance necessity.

7. **Market Confidence:** A well-executed performance evaluation system contributes to building confidence in the ETF market, which is crucial for its growth and stability.

Methods of Performance Evaluation:

Various quantitative methods are commonly deployed to evaluate the performance of ETFs, offering a range of insights into how an ETF is performing over time.

1. **Return Analysis:** The most straightforward method involves calculating the returns generated by an ETF over various time frames, including daily, monthly, yearly, or since inception.

2. **Tracking Error:** For ETFs intended to track an index, the tracking error measures the standard deviation of the difference between the ETF's returns and its benchmark index's returns. A lower tracking error indicates a higher precision in tracking the index.

3. **Expense Ratios:** The total annual costs of managing and operating an ETF, often expressed as a percentage of average net assets, are compared among similar ETFs. Lower expense ratios are typically preferred.

4. **Beta and Alpha:** Beta measures the ETF's sensitivity to market movements, while alpha indicates the ETF's ability to beat the market adjusted for risk. A high alpha is generally sought-after by investors looking for superior performance.

5. **Sharpe Ratio:** This risk-adjusted performance measure evaluates the return of an ETF relative to its risk (standard deviation). It helps determine whether higher returns are due to smart investment decisions or simply taking on more risk.

6. **Sortino Ratio:** Similar to the Sharpe Ratio, but this measure only considers downside risk, which is more relevant to investors who are concerned about negative returns.

7. **Jensen's Alpha:** Also known as the Jensen's Performance Index, it measures the average return on a portfolio or fund above or below that predicted by the capital asset pricing model (CAPM) given the portfolio's or fund's beta and the average market return.

8. **Morningstar Rating System:** Involves stars awarded based on past performance, adjusting for risk and sales charge or loads. ETFs are ranked within their categories to provide a comparative performance metric.

9. **Fama-French Three-Factor Model:** It expands on CAPM by adding size risk (small minus big, or SMB) and value risk (high book-to-market minus low, or HML) to the market risk factor in explaining returns.

10. **Active Share:** For actively managed ETFs, the Active Share measure quantifies how much the fund's holdings differ from the benchmark index, valuable in assessing how portfolio decisions are impacting performance.

Advanced data analytics tools, such as machine learning and AI, are also being used to incorporate a plethora of market data points and provide a more in-depth analysis, considering factors like market sentiment, news impact, and more complex risk-return profiles.

In practice, performance evaluation of ETFs frequently combines several of these methods to build a holistic understanding, as each metric offers a different perspective on the fund’s operations and success in meeting its investment objectives. The continuous, sophisticated performance analysis is indispensable for enhancing the ETF market's efficiency and aligning investment products with investors' expectations.
explain DEA in full details

Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) is a non-parametric method in operations research and economics for the estimation of production frontiers, which is used to empirically measure productive efficiency of decision making units (DMUs). Unlike parametric techniques that require the assumption of a specific functional form for the production process, DEA is based on mathematical programming and is used to assess the efficiency of multiple entities (e.g., hospitals, schools, businesses, etc.) that produce multiple outputs by consuming multiple inputs.

Originating from the work of Charnes, Cooper, and Rhodes in 1978, DEA centers around the concept of relative efficiency, which compares each DMU to a constructed "efficient frontier". This frontier is composed of the most efficient DMUs within the dataset, and efficiency is measured relative to this frontier.

Key Concepts and Assumptions of DEA:

1. **Decision Making Unit (DMU):** A DMU is any entity that converts inputs into outputs. In DEA, performance evaluation is carried out by comparing similar DMUs.

2. **Input and Output:** Inputs are the resources used in the production or service process, whereas outputs are the goods or services produced.

3. **Efficiency:** In DEA, efficiency is commonly categorized into technical efficiency, allocative efficiency, and economic or cost efficiency. Technical efficiency addresses the quantity of outputs that can be produced from a given set of inputs. Allocative efficiency considers the cost of inputs and value of outputs, ensuring that resources are used optimally to minimize costs or maximize profits. Economic efficiency is a combination of both technical and allocative efficiency.

4. **Efficient Frontier:** The efficient frontier is a boundary constructed from the observed set of inputs and outputs that represents the best performance observed among the DMUs. A DMU is deemed efficient if it lies on the frontier.

5. **Relative Efficiency:** The efficiency of a DMU is measured in relation to the efficient frontier. DMUs below the frontier are considered relatively less efficient.

DEA Models:

DEA is implemented through different models, with two principal variations:

1. **Input-Oriented Models:** These minimize inputs while keeping output levels constant. The focus is on how much a DMU can reduce input usage while still producing the same level of outputs.

2. **Output-Oriented Models:** These maximize outputs without increasing the level of inputs. The focus is on how much a DMU can expand outputs without changing the amount of inputs.

Each of these models can operate under the assumption of either constant returns to scale (CRS) or variable returns to scale (VRS). CRS assumes that an increase in inputs leads to a proportional increase in outputs, suitable for DMUs operating at an optimal scale. VRS allows for non-proportional increases in outputs when inputs increase and is suitable for DMUs that might not be operating at their most efficient scale.

The DEA Process:

1. **Data Collection:** Essential to DEA is the collection of accurate and quantitative input and output data for each DMU.

2. **Model Selection:** Choose between input or output orientation and decide between CRS or VRS assumptions, based on the nature of the DMUs and the objectives of the analysis.

3. **Linear Programming:** DEA constructs a piecewise surface (or frontier) using linear programming to envelop all the data points.

4. **Efficiency Score Calculation:** Each DMU receives an efficiency score from zero to one (or expressed as a percentage). An efficiency score of one (or 100%) means the DMU is on the efficiency frontier (efficient), while a score less than one indicates inefficiency.

5. **Analysis of Results:** The efficiency scores and the position of DMUs relative to the efficient frontier allow for the identification of inefficiencies, and excesses or shortfalls in inputs and outputs.

6. **Benchmarking and Improvement:** Inefficient DMUs can benchmark against those on the efficient frontier to identify areas for improvement, and strategies can be developed to move towards the efficient frontier.

DEA has various applications across different sectors and can be very insightful for performance evaluations and benchmarking. However, it also has limitations, such as being sensitive to outliers, requiring a balanced choice of inputs and outputs, and not incorporating randomness or noise in data in the way that stochastic frontier analysis (SFA) might. Despite these limitations, DEA remains a powerful tool for efficiency analysis when used appropriately.

this is research title:
Performance Evaluation of Exchange Traded Funds of Tehran Stock Exchange using Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) and Range Adjusted Measure (RAM): A Super-Efficiency Approach. Explain the research gaps this research fills.

The research title implies that the study is focusing on evaluating the performance of ETFs listed on the Tehran Stock Exchange (TSE) using advanced operational research techniques, namely Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) and the Range Adjusted Measure (RAM), within the context of a super-efficiency framework. Let's break down the potential research gaps that this study could aim to fill:

1. **Application of DEA in ETF Performance:** DEA is extensively used in various fields for efficiency analysis, but its application in the specific context of ETF performance might be limited, especially within the Iranian financial market. This research could be filling the gap by applying DEA to reinforce the methodology for ETF performance evaluation in Iran.

2. **Integration of RAM in DEA:** RAM is a variation of DEA that adjusts the traditional model to account for the range of data in inputs and outputs, which may provide a different efficiency perspective. Utilization of the RAM model in evaluating ETFs might be a novel approach that this research is introducing to the area.

3. **Focus on the Tehran Stock Exchange:** There could be a scarcity of studies that concentrate on the performance of ETFs specifically within the TSE. This research would contribute to the local knowledge base and offer insights that are contextualized to the Iranian market conditions.

4. **Super-Efficiency Models:** Super-efficiency in DEA extends the conventional model by allowing for efficiency scores greater than one, which can differentiate among efficient DMUs. This could provide a more nuanced assessment of performance, especially for ETFs, which are often close competitors. The exploration of super-efficiency in the context of TSE-listed ETFs could be a significant contribution to the literature.

5. **ETF Benchmarking in Iran:** A formal benchmarking process for ETFs using efficiency analysis methods might be underdeveloped or non-existent in the context of the TSE. This research could establish a methodological foundation for systematic ETF benchmarking.

6. **Comparative Analysis Using DEA and RAM:** Performing parallel evaluations using both DEA and RAM on the same set of ETFs might highlight how methodological differences can alter the assessment of efficiency and performance. This comparative analysis could provide a gap in existing research methodology literature.

7. **Methodological Advancement in Financial Analysis:** By integrating these methods into financial performance evaluation, the study could offer a forward step in the methodological approach to financial analysis, providing a template for future studies to build on.

8. **Policy Implications and Best Practices:** Given the unique economic and regulatory setting of Iran's financial markets, applying these quantitative methods could yield insights that inform policy and industry best practices, particularly enhancing transparency and investor trust in the financial instruments offered on the TSE.

9. **Educational and Academic Implications:** Finally, if this area remains relatively unexplored academically, performing an in-depth study with a methodologically rigorous approach could fill an educational gap and become a reference for academic and practical training in the area of investment funds and efficiency analysis in Iran.

By addressing these potential gaps, the research aims to provide a more sophisticated framework for ETF performance evaluation, adapted to the Iranian market, thereby contributing to its operational efficiency, regulatory oversight, and the facilitation of informed investment decisions.

summarize this:
The methodology chapter is a vital component of any research study as it describes the research design and the approach adopted to answer the research questions. In this chapter, the methods used in research, including research design, data collection methods, sample selection, variables and criteria, and analytical techniques are presented in detail.
In this chapter, the methodology used to evaluate the pharmaceutical companies of Tehran Stock Exchange is presented using network data coverage analysis. This study aims to evaluate the efficiency of pharmaceutical companies admitted to the Tehran Stock Exchange by using network DEA, a quantitative method that considers the interdependence between units.
The methodology chapter begins with a discussion of the research design chosen for this study and the rationale behind this choice. This section will include a discussion on the strengths and weaknesses of the chosen research design and how it aligns with the research questions and objectives.
Next, this chapter describes the data collection methods used in the research. To collect data, the researcher will use secondary data sources, especially the financial reports of pharmaceutical companies admitted to the Tehran Stock Exchange. The sample selection process will also include the criteria used to select the sample of pharmaceutical companies.
Finally, this chapter presents the analytical techniques used in the research. The main technique used in this thesis is network DEA, which considers interdependence between units.
Overall, this chapter provides an overview of the methodology used in this study and highlights the key decisions made by the researcher and how they align with the research questions and objectives.

The methodology chapter of this research is essential for detailing how the study was conducted and how the research questions will be addressed. It elaborately covers the research design, data collection methods, sample selection procedures, variables and criteria, and the analysis techniques employed.

The chapter outlines the use of network Data Envelopment Analysis (DEA) to assess the efficiency of pharmaceutical companies listed on the Tehran Stock Exchange, accounting for the interdependencies among units. It starts by justifying the chosen research design, discussing its pros and cons in relation to the research goals.

For data collection, the study relies primarily on secondary data, particularly financial reports of the selected pharmaceutical companies. Criteria for choosing these companies are explained, ensuring that the sample selection aligns with the objectives of the study.

The chapter then discusses network DEA as the principal analytical technique, which is significant for evaluating the interlinked operations typical of pharmaceutical companies.

In summary, this methodology chapter provides an in-depth explanation of the approach taken in the study to accurately evaluate the performance of pharmaceutical companies on the Tehran Stock Exchange, demonstrating how each methodological choice is congruent with the study's overarching questions and aims.

extend this:
پژوهش حاضر از حیث هدف از دسته پژوهش‌های توسعه‌ای، و از حیث روش گرد‌آوری داده‌ها از نوع پژوهش‌های کتابخانه‌ای است. از نظر روش‌ تجزیه و تحلیل داده‌ها نیز، پژوهش حاضر از مدلسازی و بهینه‌سازی ریاضی استفاده می‌کند.

پژوهش حاضر با هدف توسعه و پیشبرد دانش در یک حوزه خاص علمی و کاربردی انجام شده است. دسته‌بندی آن در زمره پژوهش‌های توسعه‌ای بیانگر تلاش برای افزایش قابلیت‌ها و بهبود شیوه‌های موجود در آن حوزه است. این نوع پژوهش‌ها معمولاً با هدف ایجاد ابزارهای نوین یا بهینه‌سازی روش‌های کاربردی انجام می‌پذیرند و به منظور رفع چالش‌ها و بهبود عملکرد در عرصه‌های مختلف صنعتی، اقتصادی، پزشکی، فناوری و غیره مورد استفاده قرار می‌گیرند.

از لحاظ روش گرد‌آوری داده‌ها، پژوهش کنونی به شیوه‌ای کتابخانه‌ای صورت گرفته است، به این معنا که داده‌ها و اطلاعات مورد نیاز از منابع معتبر کتابخانه‌ای و پایگاه‌های اطلاعاتی به دست آمده است. استفاده از مقالات علمی پژوهشی، کتب تخصصی، پایان‌نامه‌ها، گزارش‌های تحلیلی و سایر منابع مکتوب به پژوهشگر این امکان را می‌دهد تا دیدگاه‌های مختلف و تحقیقات قبلی را جمع‌آوری، مرور و تحلیل نماید، و بر این اساس به ساخت دانش جدید نائل آید.

در رابطه با روش تجزیه و تحلیل داده‌ها، این پژوهش از مدلسازی ریاضی و بهینه‌سازی بهره می‌برد که یک روش کمّی برای فهم و حل مسائل پیچیده می‌باشد. این رویکرد امکان مدل‌سازی دقیق فرآیندها، پدیده‌ها و سیستم‌های مرتبط با موضوع تحقیق را فراهم می‌کند و به پژوهشگر اجازه می‌دهد تا عملکرد مختلف سناریوها را در شرایط متفاوت شبیه‌سازی و تحلیل نماید. استفاده از تکنیک‌های بهینه‌سازی ریاضی مثل برنامه‌ریزی خطی، غیرخطی، ترکیبی و شبکه‌ای داده‌ها، پژوهشگر را مجهز به ابزاری می‌نماید که با آن می‌تواند بهینه‌ترین راه‌حل‌ها و استراتژی‌ها را برای مسائل مورد مطالعه خود پیشنهاد دهد.

این پژوهش با تکیه‌ بر این ابزارها و روش‌ها قادر است پاسخ‌هایی معتبر و ساختاریافته به سؤالات تحقیق ارائه دهد و درک پیچیدگی‌ها و جزئیات موضوع مورد نظر را تسهیل نماید، و بدین ترتیب به توسعه دانش و نوآوری در حوزه مربوطه مساهمت کند.
